##NAMES=Sulfatoredmondite
##RRUFFID=R250070
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[Pb_8_O_2_Zn(OH)_6_](SO_4_)_4_·6H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Redmond mine, Haywood County, North Carolina, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless, transparent stout prisms with wedge-shaped terminations.  It occurs together with hydroredmondite (R250068) and redmondite  (R250069) in a highly unusual secondary assemblage that comprises a variety of rare Pb-Zn-Cu sulfates, thiosulfates, and carbonates. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2023)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250070
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[Pb_8.009_O_2_Zn_0.99_(OH)_6_](SO_4_)_3.11_(S_2_O_3_)_0.89_·6H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 17.294 b: 7.3668 c: 12.7271 alpha: 90 beta: 110.622 gamma: 90 volume: 1517.5 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
